2604.10696.title: Camyla: Scaling Autonomous Research in Medical Image Segmentation
2604.10696.paper_summary: We present Camyla, a system for fully autonomous research within the scientific domain of medical image segmentation. Camyla transforms raw datasets into literature-grounded research proposals, executable experiments, and complete manuscripts without human intervention. Autonomous experimentation over long horizons poses three interrelated challenges: search effort drifts toward unpromising directions, knowledge from earlier trials degrades as context accumulates, and recovery from failures collapses into repetitive incremental fixes. To address these challenges, the system combines three coupled mechanisms: Quality-Weighted Branch Exploration for allocating effort across competing proposals, Layered Reflective Memory for retaining and compressing cross-trial knowledge at multiple granularities, and Divergent Diagnostic Feedback for diversifying recovery after underperforming trials. The system is evaluated on CamylaBench, a contamination-free benchmark of 31 datasets constructed exclusively from 2025 publications, under a strict zero-intervention protocol across two independent runs within a total of 28 days on an 8-GPU cluster. Across the two runs, Camyla generates more than 2,700 novel model implementations and 40 complete manuscripts, and surpasses the strongest per-dataset baseline selected from 14 established architectures, including nnU-Net, on 22 and 18 of 31 datasets under identical training budgets, respectively (union: 24/31). Senior human reviewers score the generated manuscripts at the T1/T2 boundary of contemporary medical imaging journals. Relative to automated baselines, Camyla outperforms AutoML and NAS systems on aggregate segmentation performance and exceeds six open-ended research agents on both task completion and baseline-surpassing frequency. These results suggest that domain-scale autonomous research is achievable in medical image segmentation.
2604.10696.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹Camyla，一個在醫學影像分割科學領域內進行完全自主研究的系統。Camyla將原始數據集轉化為以文獻為基礎的研究提案、可執行的實驗和完整的手稿，而無需人類干預。長期的自主實驗面臨三個相互關聯的挑戰：搜索努力偏向不具前景的方向、早期試驗的知識隨著背景的累積而退化，以及從失敗中恢復變成重複的增量修正。為了解決這些挑戰，該系統結合了三個耦合機制：質量加權分支探索，用於在競爭提案之間分配努力；分層反思記憶，用於在多個粒度上保留和壓縮跨試驗知識；以及分歧診斷反饋，用於在表現不佳的試驗後多樣化恢復。該系統在CamylaBench上進行評估，這是一個由2025年出版物專門構建的31個數據集的無污染基準，在一個嚴格的零干預協議下，通過兩次獨立運行，在一個8-GPU集群上總共進行了28天的測試。在這兩次運行中，Camyla生成了超過2,700個新模型實現和40篇完整手稿，並在相同的訓練預算下，分別在31個數據集中的22和18個數據集上超越了從14個已建立架構中選出的最強每數據集基準，包括nnU-Net（聯合：24/31）。資深人類審稿人在當代醫學影像期刊的T1/T2邊界對生成的手稿進行評分。與自動化基準相比，Camyla在整體分割性能上超越了AutoML和NAS系統，並在任務完成和超越基準的頻率上超過了六個開放式研究代理。這些結果表明，在醫學影像分割領域，實現領域規模的自主研究是可行的。
2604.10696.author: Yifan Gao et.al.
2604.10696.authors: Yifan Gao, Haoyue Li, Feng Yuan, Xin Gao, Weiran Huang, Xiaosong Wang
